auth.login_description: Sign in with your Twitch account to use TwitchChatBot.

setup.title: Initial Setup
setup.subtitle: Configure TwitchChatBot for your server.
setup.success: Setup complete! Please log in now.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.no_tenants: You have no channels configured yet.

maintenance.title: Maintenance
maintenance.default_message: The system is currently under maintenance. Please try again later.

cookie.message: This website only uses technically necessary cookies for authentication.

admin_overview.title: Admin Overview
admin_overview.p1: Here you can see all registered tenants (streamer channels) and the status of their bot instances in real time.
admin_overview.p2: Start/Stop: Manually start or stop the bot for a specific channel.
admin_overview.p3: Status LED: Shows the current connection status of the bot (online, offline, error).
admin_overview.p4: LIVE: The channel is currently streaming – the bot is actively monitoring the chat.

admin_user_mgmt.title: User Management
admin_user_mgmt.p1: All registered users who have signed in via Twitch.
admin_user_mgmt.p2: Suspend: Blocks login for this user. Existing sessions end on the next request.
admin_user_mgmt.p3: Kick: Immediately invalidates all active sessions without permanently blocking the account.
admin_user_mgmt.p4: Change role: Click the shield button to promote a user to admin (blue) or revoke admin rights (grey). At least one admin must always remain. Your own role cannot be changed.

admin_approval.title: Channel Approval
admin_approval.p1: New streamers must be approved before use. Here you can see all pending requests.
admin_approval.p2: Approve: Activates the channel – the bot can then be started.
admin_approval.p4: Revoke: Revokes a previously granted approval and stops the bot automatically.

admin_name_scan.title: Name Scan Filters (Admin)
admin_name_scan.p1: Here you define platform-wide name patterns that identify suspicious Twitch accounts (e.g. bot names, known troll patterns).
admin_name_scan.p2: Create filter rule: Enter name, description, and the desired action on a match.
admin_name_scan.p3: Active/Inactive: Inactive filters are not executed by any tenant, even if they are logged in.
admin_name_scan.p4: Tenants can individually decide which filters they want to use via the Name Scan page of their dashboard.

admin_settings.title: Admin Settings — Setup Guide
admin_settings.p1: ① Register Twitch App: Go to dev.twitch.tv/console → "Register Your Application". Set Redirect URI to https://your-domain.com/auth/callback. Then enter Client ID and Client Secret here.
admin_settings.p2: ② Generate Bot Token (recommended): Save Client ID + Secret first, then click "Generate Bot Token" and log in as your bot Twitch account. Token is saved automatically and can be auto-refreshed at any time since it is linked to your own app credentials.
admin_settings.p3: Alternative — manual via twitchtokengenerator.com: Access Token → Bot Token field, Refresh Token → Refresh Token field. Important: These tokens use twitchtokengenerator.com's Client ID — auto-refresh will fail with your credentials.
admin_settings.p4: Required bot scopes: chat:read chat:edit channel:moderate moderator:manage:banned_users moderator:manage:warnings

tenant_list.title: Channel Overview: Explanations
tenant_list.p1: Add channel: Enter the Twitch channel name (lowercase). The channel must first be approved by an admin before the bot joins.
tenant_list.p2: "Pending": The channel has been created but not yet approved by an admin. The bot is not participating yet.
tenant_list.p3: Add moderators: Open the channel → Settings → Moderators. You can grant other users access to your channel (view or edit).
tenant_list.p4: Bot status (LED): Green = bot online; Red = bot offline or error; Gray = unknown. The bot connects automatically when the channel is approved.

tenant_settings.title: Settings: Explanations
tenant_settings.li1: Shared Bot — the central TCB bot account is used for your channel. No own credentials needed.
tenant_settings.li2: Own Bot Account — your own Twitch account acts as the bot. Requires an OAuth token for the bot account.
tenant_settings.li3: Own Bot + App — fully own Twitch application (Client ID + Secret). For advanced users.
tenant_settings.p_bot_token: Bot Token: The OAuth token of the bot account. Generated at twitchtokengenerator.com (Scopes: chat:read, chat:edit, channel:moderate).
tenant_settings.p_mods: Moderators: Users added as moderators can either only view (Viewer) or manage filters/bans (Editor). No Twitch mod status required.
tenant_settings.p_stream: Stream Awareness: When active, the bot changes its behavior depending on whether the stream is live (e.g. more aggressive filters during livestreams).
tenant_settings.p_retention: Data Retention: Specifies how many days filter hits and action logs are stored. Older entries are automatically deleted.

tenant_mods.title: Moderators
tenant_mods.p1: Here you manage TCB moderators for your channel. These are not Twitch moderators, but users who have access to this dashboard.
tenant_mods.p2: Viewer: Can view statistics and ban lists, but cannot make changes.
tenant_mods.p3: Editor: Can manage bans, filters, and commands.
tenant_mods.p4: Search by Twitch username or enter the Twitch ID manually.

shared_bans.title: Shared Bans
shared_bans.p1: Connect your channel with other streamers to automatically synchronize bans.
shared_bans.p2: Send invitation: Enter the channel name of another TCB user and send an invitation. Once accepted, new bans from both channels will be transferred to each other.
shared_bans.p3: Role: As source you share bans, as target you receive bans from the partner channel.
shared_bans.p4: Status: Pending = invitation not yet accepted · Active = synchronization running.

ban_list.title: Ban List: Explanations
ban_list.p1: Permanent vs. Timeout: Permanent = permanent ban from chat. Timeout = temporary mute for a specific number of seconds (default: 600 = 10 minutes).
ban_list.p2: Username vs. User ID: Normally enter the Twitch username. The Twitch User ID (numeric) is optional and available in the advanced section — useful if the user changed their name.
ban_list.p3: Failover Protection (🛡): The shield symbol means the ban is also stored directly on the Twitch platform and remains active even if the bot was briefly offline.
ban_list.p4: CSV Export: Downloads the current ban list as a CSV file. Suitable for backups or importing into other tools.
ban_list.p5: Bulk Unban: Select multiple entries (checkbox) and then click "Unban selected".

name_scan.title: Name Scan
name_scan.p1: The name scan detects suspicious Twitch accounts based on their usernames (e.g. known troll patterns).
name_scan.p2: Opt-in: Choose which admin-defined filter rules should be active for your channel. Filters that are not activated will be ignored.
name_scan.p3: Auto-Ban: If enabled, matches will be automatically banned without manual confirmation.
name_scan.p4: Apply all: Retroactively bans all known matches of this filter in your channel.

commands.title: Commands: Explanations
commands.p2: Cooldown (Global): How many seconds after anyone's last use the command can be used again. Prevents spam.
commands.p3: Cooldown (User): How many seconds an individual user must wait before using the command again.
commands.p4: Response Template: Available placeholders: {user} = caller's name, {args} = provided parameters.
commands.p5: Action Type: What the bot does when the command is executed. Click the ⓘ icon next to "Action" in the form for details on each type.
